title: Execution contract exists before implementation code
summary: No feature implementation may be declared implementation-entry ready without an execution-contract entry derived from the active governed corpus.

title: First code slice shall be governance-aware baseline shell signal historian only
summary: The first code sprint is intentionally narrow and excludes output actuation, remote command execution, and full rules/sequences behavior.

title: Output actuation scope remains deferred until Sprint 2 closure
summary: The package still needs tighter execution-contract and invariant closure around arbitration, authorization, and observed mismatch proof before physical output code begins.

title: Remote supervision stays observation-only for first implementation entry
summary: Remote parity is still conceptually governed, but implementation entry remains blocked until command authorization and event evidence closure are complete.

title: Rules and sequences remain semantically closed but contract-light
summary: The semantics are mostly stable, but the next package should deepen invariant and worked-example coverage before these areas are treated as implementation-entry ready.

title: Executive summary becomes a required package artifact
summary: Every package revision shall include a root-level executive summary and a generated governance status report so a human can catch up quickly.
